audit_status.review_note: Reviewed as Table of Nations for genealogical structure, nations/languages/lands language, Nimrod, Babel/Shinar references, and spread of Noah’s sons. This chapter is intentionally lean because much of the text is name-list genealogy.

verses.restored: These are the generations of the sons of Noah: Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Sons were born to them after the flood.

familiar.text: Now these are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth...

notes.title: Table of Nations
notes.body: Genesis 10 maps peoples and lands through Noah’s sons, setting up the Babel story in Genesis 11.

verses.restored: The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.

familiar.text: The sons of Japheth; Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan...

verses.restored: The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.

familiar.text: And the sons of Gomer; Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.

verses.restored: The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.

familiar.text: And the sons of Javan; Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.

verses.restored: From these the coastland peoples spread in their lands, each with its tongue, by their families, in their nations.

familiar.text: By these were the isles of the Gentiles divided in their lands; every one after his tongue...

notes.title: Tongue, family, nation
notes.body: The chapter organizes peoples by land, language, family, and nation.

verses.restored: The sons of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.

familiar.text: And the sons of Ham; Cush, and Mizraim, and Phut, and Canaan.

verses.restored: The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.

familiar.text: And the sons of Cush; Seba, and Havilah, and Sabtah...

verses.restored: Cush fathered Nimrod. He began to be a mighty one on the earth.

familiar.text: And Cush begat Nimrod: he began to be a mighty one in the earth.

notes.title: Nimrod
notes.body: Nimrod is introduced as a mighty figure, using gibbor language.

verses.restored: He was a mighty hunter before the Eternal. Therefore it is said, Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the Eternal.

familiar.text: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD: wherefore it is said...

notes.title: Before the Eternal
notes.body: The phrase can be neutral or carry evaluative force. The text preserves the ambiguity around Nimrod’s power.

verses.restored: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Akkad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

familiar.text: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad... in the land of Shinar.

notes.title: Babel and Shinar
notes.body: Babel and Shinar prepare the reader for the tower narrative in Genesis 11.

verses.restored: From that land he went out to Asshur and built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah,

familiar.text: Out of that land went forth Asshur, and builded Nineveh...

verses.restored: and Resen between Nineveh and Calah; that is the great city.

familiar.text: And Resen between Nineveh and Calah: the same is a great city.

verses.restored: Mizraim fathered Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,

familiar.text: And Mizraim begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim...

verses.restored: Pathrusim, Casluhim, from whom the Philistines came, and Caphtorim.

familiar.text: And Pathrusim, and Casluhim, out of whom came Philistim, and Caphtorim.

verses.restored: Canaan fathered Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,

familiar.text: And Canaan begat Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,

verses.restored: the Jebusite, the Amorite, the Girgashite,

familiar.text: And the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgasite,

verses.restored: the Hivite, the Arkite, the Sinite,

familiar.text: And the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,

verses.restored: the Arvadite, the Zemarite, and the Hamathite. Afterward the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.

familiar.text: And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite: and afterward were the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.

verses.restored: The boundary of the Canaanites was from Sidon, toward Gerar as far as Gaza, toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.

familiar.text: And the border of the Canaanites was from Sidon... unto Gaza...

verses.restored: These are the sons of Ham, by their families, by their tongues, in their lands, in their nations.

familiar.text: These are the sons of Ham, after their families, after their tongues...

verses.restored: To Shem also, father of all the sons of Eber, elder brother of Japheth, children were born.

familiar.text: Unto Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber...

notes.title: Eber
notes.body: Eber is important for later Hebrew identity language.

verses.restored: The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.

familiar.text: The children of Shem; Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad...

verses.restored: The sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.

familiar.text: And the children of Aram; Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.

verses.restored: Arpachshad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.

familiar.text: And Arphaxad begat Salah; and Salah begat Eber.

verses.restored: To Eber were born two sons. The name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided; and his brother’s name was Joktan.

familiar.text: And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg; for in his days was the earth divided...

notes.title: Peleg
notes.body: Peleg is linked with division, preparing for the scattering and division themes.

verses.restored: Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,

familiar.text: And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,

verses.restored: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,

familiar.text: And Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,

verses.restored: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,

familiar.text: And Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,

verses.restored: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.

familiar.text: And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these were the sons of Joktan.

verses.restored: Their dwelling was from Mesha as you go toward Sephar, the mountain of the east.

familiar.text: And their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou goest unto Sephar a mount of the east.

verses.restored: These are the sons of Shem, by their families, by their tongues, in their lands, by their nations.

familiar.text: These are the sons of Shem, after their families, after their tongues...

verses.restored: These are the families of the sons of Noah, according to their generations, in their nations. From these the nations spread over the earth after the flood.

familiar.text: These are the families of the sons of Noah, after their generations, in their nations...

notes.title: Spread after the flood
notes.body: The chapter ends with the spreading of nations, setting up the concentrated city-building of Babel.
